edges.description: Progressive glomerular impairment manifests as renal insufficiency and end-stage kidney disease.
Chronic tubular and glomerular injury produce nephropathic disease.
Corneal cystine crystal deposition causes photophobia.
Corneal crystal disease contributes to corneal opacity.
Chronic corneal involvement can present as band keratopathy.
Ocular cystine crystal disease can impair vision.
Eye involvement beyond the cornea can include retinopathy.
Thyroid involvement causes hypothyroidism.
Gonadal involvement contributes to hypogonadism.
Gonadal endocrine dysfunction can delay puberty.
Male gonadal involvement can result in azoospermia.
Pancreatic involvement can impair glucose handling.
Pancreatic involvement can alter endocrine pancreas function.
Pancreatic endocrine dysfunction can manifest as diabetes mellitus.
Muscle involvement contributes to cystinotic myopathy.
Myopathic involvement causes progressive muscle weakness.
Muscle involvement can produce myopathic abnormalities on electromyography.
Neuromuscular involvement can manifest as hypotonia.
Neuromuscular involvement can impair gait.
Gastrointestinal and muscle involvement can produce swallowing difficulty.
Gastrointestinal involvement contributes to feeding difficulties.
Gastrointestinal involvement contributes to vomiting.
Gastrointestinal dysmotility can contribute to constipation.
Gastrointestinal and pancreatic involvement can contribute to malabsorption.
Pancreatic involvement can impair exocrine pancreatic function.
Liver involvement can manifest as hepatomegaly.
Hepatic and portal-system involvement can manifest as splenomegaly.
Hepatobiliary involvement can contribute to portal hypertension.
Nervous-system involvement can affect neurocognitive development.
Nervous-system involvement can contribute to language impairment.
Nervous-system involvement can manifest as cranial nerve dysfunction.
Multisystem cystinosis burden can contribute to fatigue.
Multisystem cystinosis can include fever, with the specific storage-to-fever intermediate unresolved.

nodes.description: Growth hormone therapy is supportive endocrine management used as needed for impaired growth in cystinosis.

Failure to export cystine causes cystine accumulation in lysosomes throughout the body. Lysosomal storage is accompanied by broader abnormalities in endolysosomal trafficking, proteolysis, lysosomal clearance, autophagy, and cellular energy balance.

nodes.description: Oral cysteamine bitartrate is disease-directed cystine-depleting therapy. Early and lifelong treatment lowers lysosomal cystine burden, delays glomerular damage and ESRD, improves growth, and reduces extrarenal complications, although it does not fully correct the Fanconi syndrome.

evidence.snippet: cystine depletion therapy (cysteamine bitartrate) significantly delays progression of glomerular damage.

nodes.description: Cystinosin deficiency causes early and severe proximal tubule dysfunction. This produces renal Fanconi syndrome with urinary losses of electrolytes, bicarbonate, minerals, glucose, amino acids, and low-molecular-weight proteins, leading to dehydration, acidosis, rickets, poor growth, and progressive renal impairment.
